args.title: B: roster binding policy
args.subject: q:must --role require a dlktk/roster binding, auto-record it, or is roster optional?

args.text: The roster fact is content-addressed and dedups (import idempotent §12); auto-record is one extra append, first-use only, fully visible in log/export — convenient, not hidden
args.author: Pragmatist

args.text: Auto-writing a fact the user did not explicitly issue is a hidden side effect: a move that silently mints a second fact violates the one-move-one-fact expectation
args.author: Purist

args.text: If roster is never written, the author↔role binding §3.1 mandates does not exist as a queryable fact: 'who played Security here?' is unanswerable and 'dlktk roster' is empty. The relation becomes decorative, defeating its §3.1 purpose
args.author: Archivist

args.text: The Q4 ethos protects decision provenance and guards against label flips; a role binding changes neither labelling nor any decision basis, so the analogy fails — capture is satisfied by auto-record, no hard gate needed
args.author: Pragmatist

args.text: dlktk's ethos is force-the-reasoning-to-be-captured (Q4); accepting a role with no recorded binding is the same implicit-state smell the tool rejects elsewhere
args.author: Archivist

args.text: Swarm setup cost: a harness spawning N personas must pre-register each per discussion or its first move aborts mid-loop; the strict gate adds a failure mode to every fresh persona
args.author: Agent

args.text: Roles never touch the evaluator (design §11); a role binding flips no labels. The Q4/Q7 force-capture gate guards against silent graph rewrites — that rationale does not transfer to pure metadata, so gating --role is friction with no soundness payoff
args.author: Pragmatist

args.text: Roster is purely optional decoration: never gates a move, never auto-written
args.author: Proposer

args.text: Auto-record the roster binding on first use of a (author,role) in a discussion
args.author: Proposer

args.text: Require an explicit roster binding before --role is accepted (reject otherwise)
args.author: Proposer

args.text: When a move carries --role, must a dlktk/roster binding already exist, be auto-recorded on first use, or is roster purely optional decoration?
args.author: chazu

args.basis: auto-record wins by structure: the force-capture analogy to Q4 fails (no labelling changes), defeating 'require'; and 'optional' makes the §3.1 roster relation decorative. Auto-record captures the author↔role binding without per-move friction; its one objection (hidden side-effect) is itself defeated by content-addressed dedup + tt visibility
